mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real sales of new vehicles and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue baselines and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic strength and rising household wealth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal produces a negative information coefficient over any 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that precedes broader economic slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in over 60% of cases.
